# 002 CUE Schema

Status: draft
Source of truth: libs/api/workflow-language/src/core/surface/surface-workflow-document-cue.ts

## Purpose

Define how PR1 uses CUE as a formalization artifact for the YAML-produced surface document shape.

PR1 does not accept CUE files as workflow authoring input.

## Normative Model

- The CUE artifact formalizes the post-YAML object shape.

- Runtime and HTTP behavior remain driven by the current YAML ingestion path.

- CUE tooling is optional until a dedicated toolchain decision is made.

<!-- generated by libs/api/workflow-language/scripts/generate-formalization-docs.ts; do not edit generated sections directly -->
<!-- generated:start -->
- CUE authoring input support: deferred.

- CUE schema execution in CI: deferred unless the PR adds the `cue` CLI deliberately.

```cue
// SurfaceWorkflowDocument formalizes the object produced by YAML parsing.
// This is a PR1 formalization artifact, not an accepted authoring input.

#StringOrStringList: string | [...string]

#RunStep: {
	run:  string
	name?: string
}

#Job: {
	needs?:  #StringOrStringList
	runner?: #StringOrStringList
	steps: [#RunStep, ...#RunStep]
}

#Trigger: {
	source: string
	event?: string
	on?:    #StringOrStringList
	with?:  {...}
	filter?: string
}

#SurfaceWorkflowDocument: {
	name: string & != ""
	triggers?: [string]: #Trigger
	runner?: #StringOrStringList
	jobs: [string]: #Job
}
```
<!-- generated:end -->

## Examples

Use CUE snippets in this document to explain shape constraints, not as accepted input examples.

## Adding Or Changing This Concept

When the YAML shape changes, update the CUE artifact in the same commit and add a drift test if the artifact is code-derived.

## Deferred Work

- CUE as a first-class surface language.
